full_definition: [G:references: G1118,G2730,G3390,G3507,G3509,G3962,G3965,G3966,G3967,G3971] 1) father of an individual 2) of God as father of his people 3) head or founder of a household, group, family, or clan 4) ancestor 4a) grandfather, forefathers — of person 4b) of people 5) originator or patron of a class, profession, or art 6) of producer, generator (fig.) 7) of benevolence and protection (fig.) 8) term of respect and honour 9) ruler or chief (spec.) exegesis:a primitive word; explanation: father , in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application translation:chief, (fore-) father(-less), [idiom] patrimony, principal. Compare names in 'Abi-'.
[G:references: G5,G912] 1) father exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to אָב H1 explanation:{ father } translation:father.
[G:references: G1080,G2590,G4491] 1) freshness, fresh green, green shoots, or greenery exegesis:from the same as אָבִיב H24 ; explanation: a green plant translation:greenness, fruit.
1) fruit, fresh, young, greening exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to אֵב H3 explanation:{ a green plant } translation:fruit.
Abagtha = "God-given" 1) one of the seven eunuchs in the Persian court of Ahasuerus exegesis:of foreign origin; explanation: Abagtha , a eunuch of Xerxes translation:Abagtha.

[G:references: G3077] 1) perish, vanish, go astray, be destroyed 1a) (Qal) 1a1) perish, die, be exterminated 1a2) perish, vanish (fig.) 1a3) be lost, strayed 1b) (Piel) 1b1) to destroy, kill, cause to perish, to give up (as lost), exterminate 1b2) to blot out, do away with, cause to vanish, (fig.) 1b3) cause to stray, lose 1c) (Hiphil) 1c1) to destroy, put to death 1c1a) of divine judgment 1c2) object name of kings (fig.) exegesis:a primitive root; explanation:properly, to wander away , i.e. lose oneself ; by implication to perish (causative, destroy) translation:break, destroy(-uction), [phrase] not escape, fail, lose, (cause to, make) perish, spend, [idiom] and surely, take, be undone, [idiom] utterly, be void of, have no way to flee.

1) to perish, vanish 1a) (P'al) shall perish 1b) (Aphel) destroy 1c) (Hophal) be destroyed exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to אָבַד H6 explanation:{properly, to wander away , i.e. lose oneself ; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)} translation:destroy, perish.
[G:references: G1821,G2147] 1) destruction exegesis:active of participle of אָבַד H6 ; explanation:(concrete) wretched or (abstract) destruction translation:perish.
1) a lost thing, something lost exegesis:from אָבַד H6 ; Compare אֲבַדֹּה H10 . explanation:concrete, something lost ; abstract, destruction , i.e. Hades translation:lost.
1) a lost thing, something lost, a perishing exegesis:the same as אֲבֵדָה H9 , miswritten for אֲבַדּוֹן H11 ; explanation: a perishing translation:destruction.
[G:references: G3] 1) place of destruction, destruction, ruin, Abaddon exegesis:intensive from אָבַד H6 ; explanation:abstract, a perishing ; concrete, Hades translation:destruction.
1) destruction exegesis:from אָבַד H6 ; explanation: a perishing translation:destruction.
1) destruction exegesis:from אָבַד H6 ; explanation: a perishing translation:destruction.

1) to be willing, consent 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to be willing 1a2) to consent, yield to, accept 1a3) to desire exegesis:a primitive root; explanation: to breathe after , i.e. (figuratively) to be acquiescent translation:consent, rest content will, be willing.
1) entreat, longing, desire exegesis:from אָבָה H14 ; explanation: longing translation:desire.

1) reed, papyrus ++++ Probably ships made of reeds or papyrus were the clipper sailing ships of those days because of their speed. exegesis:from אָבָה H14 (in the sense of bending toward); explanation: the papyrus translation:swift.
[G:references: G2351] 1) Oh!, Woe!, (exclamation of pain — indicates desire or uneasiness) exegesis:from אָבָה H14 (in the sense of desiring); explanation: want translation:sorrow.
[G:references: G596,G5336] 1) crib, manger, feeding trough exegesis:from אָבַס H75 ; explanation: a manger or stall translation:crib.
1) slaughter, flesh, meat, slaughtered meat exegesis:from an unused root (apparently meaning to turn); explanation: brandishing of a sword translation:point.
1) watermelon, Egyptian fruit exegesis:of uncertain derivation; explanation: a melon (only plural) translation:melon.
Abi = "my father" 1) mother of Hezekiah (cf H029) exegesis:from אָב H1 ; fatherly; explanation: Abi , Hezekiah's mother translation:Abi.
Abiel = "El (God) is (my) father" 1) Saul's grandfather exegesis:from אָב H1 and אֵל H410 ; father (i.e. possessor) of God; explanation: Abiel , the name of two Israelites translation:Abiel.
Abiasaph = "my father has gathered" 1) son (descendant) of Korah exegesis:from אָב H1 and אָסַף H622 ; father of gathering (i.e. gatherer); explanation: Abiasaph , an Israelite translation:Abiasaph.

[G:references: G3501,G3936] 1) fresh, young barley ears, barley 2) month of ear-forming, of greening of crop, of growing green Abib, month of exodus and passover (March or April) exegesis:from an unused root (meaning to be tender); explanation: green , i.e. a young ear of grain ; hence, the name of the month Abib or Nisan translation:Abib, ear, green ears of corn (not maize).

Abi Gibon = "father of Gibeon" 1) site of great Bamah, location of the tabernacle of the Lord in the high place exegesis:from אָב H1 and גִּבְעוֹן H1391 ; father (i.e. founder) of Gibon; explanation: Abi-Gibon , perhaps an Israelite translation:father of Gibeon.

Abigail = "my father is joy" 1) wife of Nabal, then of David 2) sister of David exegesis:or shorter אֲבִיגַל H ; from אָב H1 and גִּיל H1524 ; father (i.e. source) of joy; explanation: Abigail or Abigal , the name of two Israelitesses translation:Abigal.
Abidan = "my father is judge" 1) a prince (ruler) of Benjamin exegesis:from אָב H1 and דִּין H1777 ; father of judgment (i.e. judge); explanation: Abidan , an Israelite translation:Abidan.

Abida or Abidah = "my father knows" 1) fourth son of Midian and grandson of Abraham by his wife Keturah (after Sarah died) exegesis:from אָב H1 and יָדַע H3045 ; father of knowledge (i.e. knowing); explanation: Abida , a son of Abraham by Keturah translation:Abida, Abidah.

[G:references: G7] Abia or Abiah or Abijah = "Jehovah is (my) father" 1) king of Judah, son and successor of Rehoboam 2) second son of Samuel 3) son of Jeroboam the first, king of Israel 4) son of Becher, a Benjamite 5) head of a priestly house (one of the 24 Levite groups) 6) head of a priestly house (after the exile) 7) wife of Hezron 8) mother of Hezekiah (cf H021) exegesis:or prolonged אֲבִיָּהוּ H ; from אָב H1 and יָהּ H3050 ; father (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; explanation: Abijah , the name of several Israelite men and two Israelitesses translation:Abiah, Abijah.

Abihu = "he is (my) father" 1) a son of Aaron destroyed for sacrificing strange fire to God exegesis:from אָב H1 and הוּא H1931 ; father (i.e. worshipper) of Him (i.e. God); explanation: Abihu , a son of Aaron translation:Abihu.

[G:references: G10] Abihud = "my father is majesty" 1) son of Bela, a Benjamite exegesis:from אָב H1 and הוֹד H1935 ; father (i.e. possessor) of renown; explanation: Abihud , the name of two Israelites translation:Abihud.

Abihail = "my father is might" 1) a Levite in Moses's time 2) a Gadite 3) father of Esther 4) wife of Abishur 5) wife of Rehoboam exegesis:or (more correctly) אֲבִיחַיִל H ; from אָב H1 and חַיִל H2428 ; father (i.e. possessor) of might; explanation: Abihail or Abichail , the name of three Israelites and two Israelitesses translation:Abihail.

Abiezrite = "my father is help" 1) one of the family of Abiezer, a descendant of Joseph's son, Manasseh exegesis:from אֲבִיעֶזֶר H44 with the article inserted; father of the Ezrite; explanation: an Abiezrite or descendant of Abiezer translation:Abiezrite.

[G:references: G102,G120,G318,G435,G560,G772,G1729,G3993,G4434,G5011] 1) in want, needy, chiefly poor, needy person 2) subject to oppression and abuse 3) needing help, deliverance from trouble, especially as delivered by God 4) general reference to lowest class exegesis:from אָבָה H14 , in the sense of want (especially in feeling); explanation: destitute translation:beggar, needy, poor (man).
1) caper-berry-of stimulating desire exegesis:from אָבָה H14 ; explanation: provocative of desire ; the caper berry (from its stimulative taste) translation:desire.
Abitub = "my father is good(ness)" 1) son of Shaharaim, a Benjamite exegesis:from אָב H1 and טוּב H2898 ; father of goodness (i.e. good); explanation: Abitub , an Israelite translation:Abitub.
Abital = "my father is (the) dew" 1) a wife of David exegesis:from אָב H1 and טַל H2919 ; father of dew (i.e. fresh); explanation: Abital , a wife of King David translation:Abital.

Abijam = "my father is the sea" or "Yah(u) is (my) father" 1) king of Judah, son and successor of Rehoboam exegesis:from אָב H1 and יָם H3220 ; father of (the) sea (i.e. seaman); explanation: Abijam (or Abijah), a king of Judah translation:Abijam.

Abimael = "my father is El (God)" 1) son of Joktan, descendant of Shem exegesis:from אָב H1 and an elsewhere unused (probably foreign) word; father of Mael (apparently some Arab tribe); explanation: Abimael , a son of Joktan translation:Abimael.

Abimelech = "Melek is father" or "my father is king" 1) king of Gerar in Abraham's time 2) king of Gath in David's time; maybe title of Philistine kings 3) son of Gideon by a concubine 4) priest, son of Abiathar exegesis:from אָב H1 and מֶלֶךְ H4428 ; father of (the) king; explanation: Abimelek , the name of two Philistine kings and of two Israelites translation:Abimelech.

Abinadab = "my father is noble" or "my father is willing" 1) a man of Gibeah who sheltered the ark 2) second son of Jesse, David's older brother 3) a son of Saul, slain with him by the Philistines exegesis:from אָב H1 and נָדַב H5068 ; father of generosity (i.e. liberal); explanation: Abinadab , the name of four Israelites translation:Abinadab.

Abinoam = "my father is delight" 1) father of Barak, descendant of Naphtali exegesis:from אָב H1 and נֹעַם H5278 ; father of pleasantness (i.e. gracious); explanation: Abinoam , an Israelite translation:Abinoam.
Ebiasaph = "my father has gathered" 1) a son or descendant of Korah exegesis:contracted from אֲבִיאָסָף H23 ; explanation: Ebjasaph , an Israelite translation:Ebiasaph.

Abiezer = "my father is help" 1) a Manassite, called "son" of Gilead, also son of Gilead's sister 2) a Benjamite, warrior of David exegesis:from אָב H1 and עֵזֶר H5829 ; father of help (i.e. helpful); explanation: Abiezer , the name of two Israelites translation:Abiezer.

Abialbon = "El (God) is my father" 1) one of David's mighty men (hero) exegesis:from אָב H1 and an unused root of uncertain derivation; probably, father of strength (i.e. valiant); explanation: Abialbon , an Israelite translation:Abialbon.

[G:references: G32,G1415,G2478,G5022] 1) strong, mighty-used only to describe God 2) the Strong-old name for God (poetic) exegesis:from אָבַר H82 ; explanation: mighty (spoken of God) translation:mighty (one).

[G:references: G102,G1413,G2316,G2479,G2480] 1) mighty, valiant 1a) of men 1b) of angels 1c) of animals 1d) (metaph) 1d1) of enemies 1d2) of princes 1d3) of sacrificial objects 1e) obstinate (fig.) exegesis:for אָבִיר H46 explanation:{ mighty (spoken of God)} translation:angel, bull, chiefest, mighty (one), stout(-hearted), strong (one), valiant.

Abiram = "my father is exalted" or "(the) Exalted One is (my) father" 1) a Reubenite, son of Eliab in exodus 2) son of Hiel the Bethelite who worked to rebuild Jericho exegesis:from אָב H1 and רוּם H7311 ; father of height (i.e. lofty); explanation: Abiram , the name of two Israelites translation:Abiram.

Abishag = "my father is a wanderer" 1) David's beautiful young nurse exegesis:from אָב H1 and שָׁגָה H7686 ; father of error (i.e. blundering); explanation: Abishag , a concubine of David translation:Abishag.

x-typo:xlit ʼĂbîyshûwac corrected to Abishua = "my father is rescue (safety), or is opulence" 1) son of Phinehas, grandson of Aaron exegesis:from אָב H1 and שׁוֹעַ H7771 ; father of plenty (i.e. prosperous); explanation: Abishua , the name of two Israelites translation:Abishua.

Abishur = "my father is a wall" 1) son of Shammai, descendant of Judah exegesis:from אָב H1 and שׁוּר H7791 ; father of (the) wall (i.e. perhaps mason); explanation: Abishur , an Israelite translation:Abishur.

Abishai = "my father is Jesse" or "my father is a gift" 1) grandson of Jesse, nephew of David via his sister Zeruiah, brother of Joab exegesis:or (shorter) אַבְשַׁי H ; from אָב H1 and שַׁי H7862 ; father of a gift (i.e. probably generous); explanation: Abishai , an Israelite translation:Abishai.

Absalom or Abishalom = "my father is peace" 1) father-in-law of Rehoboam 2) third son of David, killer of first-born son Amnon, also leader of revolt against his father-David exegesis:or (shortened) אַבְשָׁלוֹם H ; from אָב H1 and שָׁלוֹם H7965 ; father of peace (i.e. friendly); explanation: Abshalom , a son of David; also (the fuller form) a later Israelite translation:Abishalom, Absalom.

[G:references: G8] Abiathar = "my father is great" 1) priest, son of Ahitub (Ahimelech), faithful to David, but later rebelled with Adonijah exegesis:contracted from אָב H1 and יָתַר H3498 ; father of abundance (i.e. liberal); explanation: Ebjathar , an Israelite translation:Abiathar.
1) to roll, turn 1a) (Hithpael) to roll, roll up, billow exegesis:a primitive root; explanation:probably to coil upward translation:mount up.

[G:references: G2354,G2400,G3996,G4133,G4727] 1) to mourn, lament 1a) (Qal) to mourn, lament 1a1) of humans 1a2) of inanimate objects (fig.) 1a2a) of gates 1a2b) of land 1b) (Hiphil) 1b1) to mourn, cause to mourn (fig.) 1c) (Hithpael) 1c1) to mourn 1c2) play the mourner exegesis:a primitive root; explanation: to bewail translation:lament, mourn.

[G:references: G93,G93,G94,G458,G1342,G3076,G3900,G4835] 1) mourning 1a) for the dead 1b) because of calamity 1c) of rites of mourning 2) mourner (subst.) 2a) for the dead 2b) for calamity exegesis:from אָבַל H56 ; explanation: lamenting translation:mourn(-er, -ing).

[G:references: G9] 1) meadow (from verb-to grow green, to withstand) exegesis:from an unused root (meaning to be grassy); explanation: a meadow translation:plain. Compare also the proper names beginning with Abel-.

1) city in northern Israel near Bethmaachah 2) the place where the ark rested in the field of Joshua at Bethshemesh exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 ; a meadow; explanation: Abel , the name of two places in Palestine. translation:Abel.

[G:references: G2805,G3601,G3806,G3997] 1) mourning 1a) for the dead 1b) for rites of mourning (metaph) 1c) mourning garb 1d) period of mourning exegesis:from אָבַל H56 ; explanation: lamentation translation:mourning.

1) truly, verily, surely 2) but, however, howbeit 3) contrariwise, nay rather (neg.) exegesis:apparently from אָבַל H56 through the idea of negation; explanation: nay , i.e. truly or yet translation:but, indeed, nevertheless, verily.

Abel Beth Maachah = "meadow of the house of Maachah" 1) city in northern Israel near Beth Maachah exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 and בַּיִת H1004 and מַעֲכָה H4601 ; meadow of Beth-Maakah; explanation: Abel of Beth-maakah , a place in Palestine translation:Abel-beth-maachah, Abel of Beth-maachah,

Abel Shittim = "meadow of acacias" 1) place in lowlands of Moab exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 and the plural of שִׁטָּה H7848 , with the article inserted; meadow of the acacias; explanation: Abel hash-Shittim , a place in Palestine translation:Abelshittim.

Abel Keramim = "meadow of the vineyards" 1) a place in Ammon exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 and the plural of כֶּרֶם H3754 ; meadow of vineyards; explanation: Abel-Keramim , a place in Palestine translation:plain of the vineyards.

Abel Meholah = "meadow of dancing" 1) a city of Issachar, birthplace of Elisha exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 and מְחֹלָה H4246 ; meadow of dancing; explanation: Abel-Mecholah , a place in Palestine translation:Abel-meholah.

Abel Maim = "meadow of waters" 1) a city in northern Israel (perhaps Abel Beth Maachah) exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 and מַיִם H4325 ; meadow of water; explanation: Abel-Majim , a place in Palestine translation:Abel-maim.

Abel Mizraim = "meadow of Egypt" 1) a place east of the Jordan (perhaps "as fertile as Egypt") exegesis:from אָבֵל H58 and מִצְרַיִם H4714 ; meadow of Egypt; explanation: Abel-Mitsrajim , a place in Palestine translation:Abel-mizraim.

[G:references: G2729,G3035,G3037,G3398,G5464] 1) stone (large or small) 1a) common stone (in natural state) 1b) stone, as material 1b1) of tablets 1b2) marble, hewn stones 1c) precious stones, stones of fire 1d) stones containing metal (ore), tool for work or weapon 1e) weight 1f) plummet (stones of destruction) also made of metal 1g) stonelike objects, eg hailstones, stony heart, ice 1h) sacred object, as memorial Samuel set up to mark where God helped Israel to defeat the Philistines 1i) (simile) 1i1) sinking in water, motionlessness 1i2) strength, firmness, solidity 1i3) commonness 1j) (metaph) 1j1) petrified with terror 1j2) perverse, hard heart exegesis:from the root of בָּנָה H1129 through the meaning to build; explanation: a stone translation:[phrase] carbuncle, [phrase] mason, [phrase] plummet, (chalk-, hail-, head-, sling-) stone(-ny), (divers) weight(-s).
1) stone 1a) a (the) stone 1b) stone, material of idols and buildings exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to אֶבֶן H68 explanation:{ a stone } translation:stone.

1) wheel, disc 1a) potter's wheel 1b) bearing-stool, midwife's stool exegesis:from the same as אֶבֶן H68 ; explanation: a pair of stones (only dual); a potter's wheel or a midwife's stool (consisting alike of two horizontal disks with a support between) translation:wheel, stool.
1) river Abana, Syrian river flowing through Damascus exegesis:perhaps feminine of אֶבֶן H68 ; stony; Compare אֲמָנָה H549 . explanation: Abanah , a river near Damascus translation:Abana.

Ebenezer = "stone of help" 1) memorial stone erected by Samuel to mark where God helped Israel to defeat the Philistines-north of Jerusalem exegesis:from אֶבֶן H68 and עֵזֶר H5828 with the article inserted; stone of the help; explanation: Eben-ha-Ezer , a place in Palestine translation:Ebenezer.
[G:references: G2223,G4735] 1) girdle, sash, waistband 1a) of high priest 1b) of other priests 1c) of high official exegesis:of uncertain derivation; explanation: a belt translation:girdle.

Abner = "my father is a lamp" 1) Saul's cousin and army captain, treacherously slain by Joab exegesis:or (fully) אֲבִינֵר H ; from אָב H1 and נִיר H5216 ; father of light (i.e. enlightening); explanation: Abner , an Israelite translation:Abner.
[G:references: G4618] 1) to feed, fatten 1a) (Qal) fattened (passive participle) exegesis:a primitive root; explanation: to fodder translation:fatted, stalled.
1) blisters, boils (from root, to swell up) exegesis:(by reduplication) from an unused root (meaning to belch forth); explanation: an inflammatory pustule (as eruption) translation:blains.
Abez = "I will make white (or miry)" 1) a city in Issachar exegesis:from an unused root probably meaning to gleam; conspicuous; explanation: Ebets , a place in Palestine translation:Abez.

Ibzan, "their whiteness (literally their tin — as white" 1) a Bethlehemite judge in time of the judges exegesis:from the same as אֲבַעְבֻּעָה H76 ; splendid; explanation: Ibtsan , an Israelite translation:Ibzan.

1) (Niphal) to wrestle, grapple (get dusty), bedust exegesis:a primitive root, probably to float away (as vapor), but used only as denominative from אָבָק H80 ; explanation: to bedust , i.e. grapple translation:wrestle.
[G:references: G2868] 1) dust 1a) on ground 1b) clouds (fig.) exegesis:from root of אָבַק H79 ; explanation: light particles (as volatile) translation:(small) dust, powder.
1) aromatic powders, powder exegesis:feminine of אָבָק H80 explanation:{ light particles (as volatile)} translation:powder.
1) (Hiphil) to fly (to move wings) exegesis:a primitive root; explanation: to soar translation:fly.
1) pinion, wing 1a) of bird (dove, eagle) 1b) of Babylonian king (fig.) exegesis:from אָבַר H82 ; explanation: a pinion translation:(long-) wing(-ed).
1) pinion, wing 1a) of bird (ostrich, eagle, dove) 1b) of God (metaph) exegesis:feminine of אֵבֶר H83 explanation:{ a pinion } translation:feather, wing.

[G:references: G11] Abraham = "father of a multitude" or "chief of multitude" 1) friend of God and founder of Hebrew nation via God's elective covenant exegesis:contracted from אָב H1 and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude; explanation: Abraham , the later name of Abram translation:Abraham.
1) (meaning dubious) -a shout made to announce Joseph's chariot 1a) command 1b) bow exegesis:probably an Egyptian word meaning explanation: kneel translation:bow the knee.
Abram = "exalted father" 1) original name of Abraham exegesis:contracted from אֲבִירָם H48 ; high father; explanation: Abram , the original name of Abraham translation:Abram.

Oboth = "waterskins" 1) undetermined site of an Israelite camp in the wilderness (perhaps at east boundary of Moab) exegesis:plural of אוֹב H178 ; water-skins; explanation: Oboth , a place in the Desert translation:Oboth.
Agee = "I shall increase" 1) father of a warrior of David exegesis:of uncertain derivation (compare אֲגַג H90 ); explanation: Age , an Israelite translation:Agee.

Agag = "I will overtop" 1) king of Amalek, spared by Saul but slain by Samuel exegesis:or אֲגָג H ; of uncertain derivation (compare אָגֵא H89 ); flame; explanation: Agag , a title of Amalekitish kings translation:Agag.
Agagite = "I will overtop" 1) said of Haman, Haman the Agagite exegesis:patrial or patronymic from אֲגַג H90 ; explanation: an Agagite or descendent (subject) of Agag translation:Agagite.

[G:references: G1197,G1860] 1) band, binding 1a) cords, bands, thongs (metaphorical of slavery) 1b) bunch of hyssop 1c) band of men, troops 1d) vault (of the heavens), firmament (binding earth to the heavens) exegesis:feminine passive participle of an unused root (meaning to bind); explanation: a band , bundle , knot , or arch translation:bunch, burden, troop.
1) nuts exegesis:prob of Persian origin; explanation: a nut translation:nut.

Agur = "gathered" 1) son of Jakeh, an author or compiler of Pr 30:1-33 exegesis:passive participle of אָגַר H103 ; gathered (i.e. received among the sages); explanation: Agur , a fanciful name for Solomon translation:Agur.

1) payment, piece, coin exegesis:from the same as אָגוּר H94 ; explanation:properly, something gathered , i.e. perhaps a grain or berry ; used only of a small (silver) coin translation:piece (of) silver.
1) drop, reserve supply, collections, stores exegesis:from an unused root (meaning to flow down or together as drops); explanation: a reservoir translation:drop.
Eglaim = "double reservoir" 1) a town in Moab exegesis:dual of אֶגֶל H96 .; a double pond; explanation: Eglajim , a place in Moab translation:Eglaim.

[G:references: G3041] 1) pool, troubled pool 1a) troubled or muddy (gloomy) pools, marshes 1b) any pool, pond 1c) swamp reeds, reeds, rush(es) exegesis:from an unused root (meaning to collect as water); explanation: a marsh ; hence a rush (as growing in swamps); hence a stockade of reeds translation:pond, pool, standing (water).
1) stagnant pond exegesis:probably from the same as אֲגַם H98 (in the sense of stagnant water); explanation:figuratively, sad translation:pond.

[G:references: G440] 1) rush, bulrush 1a) used as cord or line (of twisted rushes or spun of rush fibre) 1b) of the lowly, insignificant (metaph) 2) sad, drooping 1a) of line of bulrushes 1b) bowing of the head (fig.) 1c) of the lowly (metaph) exegesis:from the same as אֲגַם H98 ; a marshy pool (others from a different root, a kettle); by implication explanation: a bulrush (as growing there); collectively a rope of bulrushes translation:bulrush, caldron, hook, rush.

explanation: to know (properly, to ascertain by seeing); used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially (including observation, care, recognition; and causatively, instruction, designation, punishment, etc.) translation:acknowledge, acquaintance(-ted with), advise, answer, appoint, assuredly, be aware, (un-) awares, can(-not), certainly, comprehend, consider, [idiom] could they, cunning, declare, be diligent, (can, cause to) discern, discover, endued with, familiar friend, famous, feel, can have, be (ig-) norant, instruct, kinsfolk, kinsman, (cause to let, make) know, (come to give, have, take) knowledge, have (knowledge), (be, make, make to be, make self) known, [phrase] be learned, [phrase] lie by man, mark, perceive, privy to, [idiom] prognosticator, regard, have respect, skilful, shew, can (man of) skill, be sure, of a surety, teach, (can) tell, understand, have (understanding), [idiom] will be, wist, wit, wot.

[G:references: G1843,G5263] 1) to know 1a) (P'al) to know 1b) (Aphel) to let someone know, communicate, inform, cause to know exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to יָדַע H3045 explanation:{ to know (properly, to ascertain by seeing); used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially (including observation, care, recognition; and causatively, instruction, designation, punishment, etc.)} translation:certify, know, make known, teach.

Jada = "He knows" 1) a Judahite, son of Onam and brother of Shammai, in the genealogy of the sons of Jerahmeel by his wife Atarah exegesis:from יָדַע H3045 ; knowing; explanation: Jada , an Israelite translation:Jada.

Jedaiah = "Jehovah has known" 1) a priest in Jerusalem 2) a man who returned with Zerubbabel 3) a ruler of the priests in the days of Jeshua 4) a post exilic priest 5) one honoured by Jehovah, perhaps one of the above exegesis:from יָדַע H3045 and יָהּ H3050 ; Jah has known; explanation: Jedajah , the name of two Israelites translation:Jedaiah.

[G:references: G1109] 1) a knower, one who has a familiar spirit 1a) a familiar spirit, soothsayer, necromancer (meton) exegesis:from יָדַע H3045 ; explanation:properly, a knowing one ; specifically, a conjurer ; (by impl) a ghost translation:wizard.

[G:references: G239] 1) Jah (Jehovah in the shortened form) 1a) the proper name of the one true God 1b) used in many compounds 1b1) names beginning with the letters 'Je' 1b2) names ending with 'iah' or 'jah' exegesis:contraction for יְהֹוָה H3068 , and meaning the same; explanation: Jah , the sacred name translation:Jah, the Lord, most vehement. Compare names in '-iah,' '-jah.'

[G:references: G1325,G1439,G1521,G3860,G5342] 1) to give, provide, ascribe, come 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to give 1a2) to set, place 1a3) to provide (with reflexive) 1a4) to ascribe (glory) 1a5) to grant, permit, come now exegesis:a primitive root; explanation: to give (whether literal or figurative); generally, to put ; imperatively (reflexive) come translation:ascribe, bring, come on, give, go, set, take.

[G:references: G591,G3308] 1) to give, provide 1a) (P'al) 1a1) to give 1a2) to place, lay (foundations) 1b) (Hithp'al) 1b1) to be given 1b2) to be paid exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to יָהַב H3051 explanation:{ to give (whether literal or figurative); generally, to put ; imperatively (reflexive) come } translation:deliver, give, lay, [phrase] prolong, pay, yield.
1) burden, lot (that which is given) exegesis:from יָהַב H3051 ; explanation:properly, what is given (by Providence), i.e. a lot translation:burden.

[G:references: G2450,G4059] 1) (Hithpael) to become a Jew (in fact or in fraud), become Judaised exegesis:denominative from a form corresponding to יְהוּד H3061 ; explanation: to Judaize , i.e. become Jewish translation:become Jews.

Jehud = "beauty" 1) a town in Dan, located between Baalath and Bene-berak and 8 miles east of Joppa exegesis:a briefer form of one corresponding to יְהוּד H3061 ; explanation: Jehud , a place in Palestine translation:Jehud.
Jahdai "whom he will place" 1) one of the family of Caleb exegesis:perhaps from a form corresponding to יְהוּד H3061 ; Judaistic; explanation: Jehdai , an Israelite translation:Jehdai.
adj f 1) the Jewess Jehudijah = Jewess n pr f 2) (CLBL) a Calebite, the wife of one named Ezra exegesis:feminine of יְהוּדִי H3064 ; explanation: Jehudijah , a Jewess translation:Jehudijah.

Jehu = "Jehovah is He" 1) the king of the northern kingdom Israel who overthrew the dynasty of Omri 2) son of Hanani and an Israelite prophet in the time of Baasha and Jehoshaphat 3) the Antothite, a Benjamite, one of David's mighty warriors 4) a descendant of Judah of the house of Hezron 5) son of Josibiah and a chief of the tribe of Simeon exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and הוּא H1931 ; Jehovah (is) He; explanation: Jehu , the name of five Israelites translation:Jehu.

Jehoahaz = "Jehovah has seized" 1) a king of Judah and son of Josiah 2) a king of the northern kingdom of Israel and son of Jehu 3) a king of Judah and son of Jehoram (Ahaziah) exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and אָחַז H270 ; Jehovah-seized; explanation: Jehoachaz , the name of three Israelites translation:Jehoahaz. Compare יוֹאָחָז H3099 .

Jehoash = "given by the Lord" 1) son of king Ahaziah and the 8th king of Judah 2) son of king Jehoahaz and the 12th king of the northern kingdom of Israel exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and (perhaps) אֵשׁ H784 ; Jehovah-fired; explanation: Jehoash , the name of two Israelite kings translation:Jehoash. Compare יוֹאָשׁ H3101 .

x-typo:lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to 1) Judah-the territory of the tribe of Judah exegesis:(Aramaic) contracted from a form corresponding to יְהוּדָה H3063 ; explanation:properly, Judah , hence, Judaea translation:Jewry, Judah, Judea.
1) Jew exegesis:(Aramaic) patrial from יְהוּד H3061 ; explanation: a Jehudaite (or Judaite), i.e. Jew translation:Jew.

[G:references: G2448,G2455] Judah = "praised" 1) the son of Jacob by Leah 2) the tribe descended from Judah the son of Jacob 3) the territory occupied by the tribe of Judah 4) the kingdom comprised of the tribes of Judah and Benjamin which occupied the southern part of Canaan after the nation split upon the death of Solomon 5) a Levite in Ezra's time 6) an overseer of Jerusalem in the time of Nehemiah 7) a Levite musician in the time of Nehemiah 8) a priest in the time of Nehemiah exegesis:from יָדָה H3034 ; celebrated; explanation: Jehudah (or Judah), the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory translation:Judah.
1) Jew exegesis:patronymically from יְהוּדָה H3063 ; explanation: a Jehudite (i.e. Judaite or Jew), or descendant of Jehudah (i.e. Judah) translation:Jew.
Jehudi = "Jew" 1) son of Nethaniah and servant of king Jehoiakim exegesis:the same as יְהוּדִי H3064 ; explanation: Jehudi , an Israelite translation:Jehudi.
1) in the Jewish language, in Hebrew exegesis:feminine of יְהוּדִי H3064 ; explanation: the Jewish (used adverbially) language translation:in the Jews' language.
Judith = "Jewess" or "praised" 1) the daughter of Beeri the Hittite and wife of Esau exegesis:the same as יְהוּדִית H3066 ; Jewess; explanation: Jehudith , a Canaanitess translation:Judith.

[G:references: G1203,G2316,G2962] Jehovah = "the existing One" 1) the proper name of the one true God 1a) unpronounced except with the vowel pointings of H0136 exegesis:from הָיָה H1961 ; (the) self-Existent or Eternal; explanation: Jehovah , Jewish national name of God translation:Jehovah, the Lord. Compare יָהּ H3050 , יְהֹוִה H3069 .

1) Jehovah-used primarily in the combination 'Lord Jehovah' 1a) equal to H03068 but pointed with the vowels of H0430 exegesis:a variation of יְהֹוָה H3068 (used after אֲדֹנָי H136 , and pronounced by Jews as אֱלֹהִים H430 , in order to prevent the repetition of the same sound, since they elsewhere pronounce יְהֹוָה H3068 as אֲדֹנָי H136 ) explanation:{ YHWH } translation:God.

x-typo:xlit yireh corrected to Jehovah-jireh = "Jehovah sees" 1) symbolic name given by Abraham to Mount Moriah in commemoration of the interposition of the angel of Jehovah who prevented the sacrifice of Isaac and provided a substitute exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and רָאָה H7200 ; Jehovah will see (to it); explanation: Jehovah-Jireh , a symbolical name for Mount Moriah translation:Jehovah-jireh.

Jehovah-nissi = "Jehovah is my banner" 1) the name given by Moses to the altar which he built in commemoration of the discomfiture of the Amalekites exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and נֵס H5251 with the prononimal suffix; Jehovah (is) my banner; explanation: Jehovah-Nissi , a symbolical name of an altar in the Desert translation:Jehovah-nissi.

Jehovah is our righteousness 1) a sacred name symbolically applied to Jerusalem and the Messiah exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and צֶדֶק H6664 with pronominal suffix; Jehovah (is) our right; explanation: Jehovah-Tsidkenu , a symbolical epithet of the Messiah and of Jerusalem translation:the Lord our righteousness.

Jehovah-shalom = "Jehovah is peace" 1) the name of a sacred altar built by Gideon in Ophrah exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and שָׁלוֹם H7965 ; Jehovah (is) peace; explanation: Jehovah-Shalom , a symbolical name of an altar in Palestine translation:Jehovah-shalom.

Jehovah-shammah = "Jehovah is there" 1) a symbolic name for Jerusalem exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and שָׁם H8033 with directive enclitic; Jehovah (is) thither; explanation: Jehovah-Shammah , a symbolic title of Jerusalem translation:Jehovahshammah.

Jehozabad = "Jehovah has endowed" 1) son of Shomer, a Moabitish woman, who murdered Joash, king of Judah 2) a Benjamite, captain of 180,000 armed men, in the days of Jehoshaphat 3) a Korhite Levite, 2nd son of Obededom, and one of the porters of the temple and of the storehouse there in the time of David exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and זָבַד H2064 ; Jehovah-endowed; explanation: Jehozabad , the name of three Israelites translation:Jehozabad. Compare יוֹזָבָד H3107 .

Jehohanan = "Jehovah has graced" 1) a priest during the high priesthood of Joiakim who returned with Zerubbabel 2) a high priest in the time of Ezra 3) a Levite priest who took part in the dedication of the wall in the time of Nehemiah 4) son of Tobiah 5) a Korhite Levite and one of the doorkeepers to the tabernacle in the time of David 6) a captain of Judah under king Jehoshaphat 7) an Ephraimite 8) an Israelite with a foreign wife in the time of Ezra 9) a Jewish captain after the fall of Jerusalem 10) the eldest son of king Josiah 11) a post-exilic prince of the line of David 12) father of Azariah, priest in Solomon's time 13) a Benjamite, one of David's mighty warriors 14) a Gadite, one of David's mighty warriors 15) a returning exile exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and חָנַן H2603 ; Jehovah-favored; explanation: Jehochanan , the name of eight Israelites translation:Jehohanan, Johanan. Compare יוֹחָנָן H3110 .

Jehoiada = "Jehovah knows" 1) father of Benaiah, David's mighty warrior 2) leader of the priests who joined David at Hebron 3) high priest at the time of Athaliah's usurpation of the throne of Judah; hid Joash, the king's son, for six years and eventually replaced him on the throne of Judah 4) second priest, or sagan, to Seraiah the high priest exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and יָדַע H3045 ; Jehovah-known; explanation: Jehojada , the name of three Israelites translation:Jehoiada. Compare יוֹיָדָע H3111 .

Jehoiachin = "Jehovah establishes" 1) king of Judah, son of Jehoiakim, and the next to last king of Judah before the Babylonian captivity; kingship lasted for 3 months and 10 days before he surrendered to Nebuchadnezzar who took him to Babylon and imprisoned him for 36 years when he was finally released exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and כּוּן H3559 ; Jehovah will establish; explanation: Jehojakin , a Jewish king translation:Jehoiachin. Compare יוֹיָכִין H3112 .

Jehoiakim = "Jehovah raises up" 1) son of Josiah and the third from the last king of Judah; subject vassel of Nebuchadnezzar who reigned for 11 years before he died a violent death either in combat or by the hands of his own subjects exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 abbreviated and קוּם H6965 ; Jehovah will raise; explanation: Jehojakim , a Jewish king translation:Jehoiakim. Compare יוֹיָקִים H3113 .

Jehoiarib = "Jehovah contends" 1) A priest in Jerusalem 2) the head of the 1st of the 24 courses of priests in David's time exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and רִיב H7378 ; Jehovah will contend; explanation: Jehojarib , the name of two Israelites translation:Jehoiarib. Compare יוֹיָרִיב H3114 .

Jehucal = "Jehovah is able" 1) a son of Shelemiah and a servant of king Zedekiah whom he sent to Jeremiah to entreat his prayers and advice exegesis:from יָכֹל H3201 ; potent; explanation: Jehukal , an Israelite translation:Jehucal. Compare יוּכַל H3116 .

Jehonadab = "Jehovah is willing" 1) a son of Rechab, chief of the Rechabites, in the time of Jehu and Ahab 2) a nephew of David exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and נָדַב H5068 ; Jehovah-largessed; explanation: Jehonadab , the name of an Israelite and of an Arab translation:Jehonadab, Jonadab. Compare יוֹנָדָב H3122 .

Jonathan or Jehonathan = "Jehovah has given" 1) a son of king Saul and a friend of David 2) a son of the high priest Abiathar and the last descendant of Eli of whom we hear 3) a nephew of David who like David slew a giant of Gath 4) an uncle of David 5) one of David's mighty warriors 6) one of David's treasurers 7) a scribe in the time of Jeremiah 8) a Levite and father of Zechariah, a priest who blew the trumpet at the dedication of the wall 9) a son or descendant of Gershom, the son of Moses, and a priest to the tribe of Dan 10) a son of Kareah and a brother of Johanan; a Judaite captain after the fall of Jerusalem 11) another Judaite father of Peleth 12) father of Ebed in the time of Ezra 13) son of Asahel in the time of Ezra 14) a priest of the family of Melicu in the time of Nehemiah 15) son of Joiada and his successor to the high priesthood in the time of Nehemiah exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and נָתַן H5414 ; Jehovah-given; explanation: Jehonathan , the name of four Israelites translation:Jonathan. Compare יוֹנָתָן H3129 .
Joseph = "Jehovah has added" 1) the eldest son of Jacob by Rachel exegesis:a fuller form of יוֹסֵף H3130 ; explanation: Jehoseph (i.e. Joseph), a son of Jacob translation:Joseph.
Jehoadah = "Jehovah has adorned" 1) a descendant of Saul through Jonathan exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and עָדָה H5710 ; Jehovah-adorned; explanation: Jehoaddah , an Israelite translation:Jehoada.

Jehoaddan = "Jehovah delights" 1) wife of king Joash and mother of king Amaziah of Judah exegesis:or יְהוֹעַדָּן H ; from יְהֹוָה H3068 and עָדַן H5727 ; Jehovahpleased; explanation: Jehoaddin or Jehoaddan , an Israelitess translation:Jehoaddan.

Jehozadak or Josedech = "Jehovah is righteous" 1) grandson of the high priest Hilkiah; son of the high priest Seraiah; and father of the high priest Joshua; he never attained the office of high priest himself because he was carried captive to Babylon by Nebuchadnezzar exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and צָדַק H6663 ; Jehovah-righted; explanation: Jehotsadak , an Israelite translation:Jehozadek, Josedech. Compare יוֹצָדָק H3136 .

Jehoram or Joram = "Jehovah is exalted" 1) son of king Jehoshaphat of Judah and himself king of Judah for 8 years; his wife was the wicked Athaliah who was probably the instigator for his returning the nation of Judah to the worship of Baal 2) son of king Ahab of the northern kingdom of Israel and king of Israel himself for 12 years; he was murdered by Jehu on the plot of land for which his father had murdered Naboth thus fulfilling the prophecy of Elijah to the very letter 3) a priest in the reign of Jehoshaphat exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and רוּם H7311 ; Jehovah-raised; explanation: Jehoram , the name of a Syrian and of three Israelites translation:Jehoram, Joram. Compare יוֹרָם H3141 .

Jehosheba = "Jehovah has sworn" 1) daughter of king Joram of Judah and wife of the high priest Jehoiada 1a) alternate spelling 'Jehoshabeath' - H03090 exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and שָׁבַע H7650 ; Jehovah-sworn; explanation: Jehosheba , an Israelitess translation:Jehosheba. Compare יְהוֹשַׁבְעַת H3090 .

Jehoshabeath = "Jehovah is an oath" 1) daughter of king Joram of Judah and wife of the high priest Jehoiada 1a) alternate spelling 'Jehosheba- H3089 exegesis:a form of יְהוֹשֶׁבַע H3089 ; explanation: Jehoshabath , an Israelitess translation:Jehoshabeath.

[G:references: G2424,G919] Joshua or Jehoshua = "Jehovah is salvation" n pr m 1) son of Nun of the tribe of Ephraim and successor to Moses as the leader of the children of Israel; led the conquest of Canaan 2) a resident of Beth-shemesh on whose land the Ark of the Covenant came to a stop after the Philistines returned it 3) son of Jehozadak and high priest after the restoration 4) governor of Jerusalem under king Josiah who gave his name to a gate of the city of Jerusalem exegesis:or יְהוֹשֻׁעַ H ; from יְהֹוָה H3068 and יָשַׁע H3467 ; Jehovah-saved; explanation: Jehoshua (i.e. Joshua), the Jewish leader translation:Jehoshua, Jehoshuah, Joshua. Compare הוֹשֵׁעַ H1954 , יֵשׁוּעַ H3442 .

[G:references: G2498] Jehoshaphat = "Jehovah has judged" n pr m 1) son of king Asa and himself king of Judah for 25 years; one of the best, most pious, and prosperous kings of Judah 2) son of Nimshi and father of king Jehu of the northern kingdom of Israel 3) son of Ahilud and chronicler under David and Solomon 4) son of Paruah and one of the 12 commissary officers under Solomon 5) a priest and trumpeter in the time of David n pr loc 6) symbolical name of a valley near Jerusalem which is the place of ultimate judgment; maybe the deep ravine which separates Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives through which the Kidron flowed exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and שָׁפַט H8199 ; Jehovah-judged; explanation: Jehoshaphat , the name of six Israelites; also of a valley near Jerusalem translation:Jehoshaphat. Compare יוֹשָׁפָט H3146 .
[G:references: G213,G829] 1) proud, arrogant, haughty exegesis:probably from the same as הַר H2022 ; explanation: elated ; hence, arrogant translation:haughty, proud.

Jehaleleel or Jehalelel = "God is praised" 1) a Calebite descendant of Judah 2) father of Azariah and a Merarite Levite in the time of Hezekiah exegesis:from הָלַל H1984 and אֵל H410 ; praising God; explanation: Jehallelel , the name of two Israelites translation:Jehalellel, Jehalelel.

[G:references: G2393,G4665] 1) a precious stone (known for its hardness) 1a) perhaps the jasper, onyx, or diamond exegesis:from הָלַם H1986 (in the sense of hardness); explanation: a precious stone , probably onyx translation:diamond.

Jahaz or Jahazah or Jahzah = "trodden down" 1) a place in Reuben east of the Dead Sea where Israel defeated Sihon king of the Amorites; site uncertain exegesis:or יַהְצָה H ; or (feminine) יַהְצָה H ; from an unused root meaning to stamp; perhaps threshing-floor; explanation: Jahats or Jahtsah , a place East of the Jordan translation:Jahaz, Jahazah, Jahzah.

Joab = "Jehovah is father" 1) son of David's sister Zeruiah and general of David's army 2) a Judaite descendant of Kenaz 3) a post exilic family exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and אָב H1 ; Jehovah-fathered; explanation: Joab , the name of three Israelites translation:Joab.

Joah = "Jehovah is brother" 1) son of Asaph and chronicler to Hezekiah 2) a Gershonite son or grandson of Zimmah 3) a Korhite, 3rd son of Obed-edom and doorkeeper in the time of David 4) son of Joahaz and chronicler to king Josiah exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and אָח H251 ; Jehovahbrothered; explanation: Joach , the name of four Israelites translation:Joah.

Joahaz or Jehoahaz = "Jehovah has grasped" 1) the father of Joah, Josiah's chronicler exegesis:a form of יְהוֹאָחָז H3059 ; explanation: Joachaz , the name of two Israelites translation:Jehoahaz, Joahaz.

[G:references: G2493] Joel = "Jehovah is God" 1) son of Pethuel and the 2nd of the 12 minor prophets with a book by his name; probably prophesied in the time of king Uzziah of Judah 2) eldest son of Samuel the prophet and father of Heman the singer 3) a Simeonite chief 4) a Reubenite 5) a chief of Gad 6) son of Izrahiah and a chief of Issachar 7) brother of Nathan of Zobah and one of David's mighty warriors 8) son of Pedaiah and a chief of the half tribe of Manasseh west of the Jordan in the time of David 9) a son of Nebo who returned with Ezra and had a foreign wife 10) a Benjamite, son of Zichri 11) a Levite 12) a Kohathite Levite in the reign of Hezekiah 13) a Gershonite Levite chief in the time of David 14) a Gershonite Levite, son of Jehiel and a descendant of Laadan; maybe same as 13 exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and אֵל H410 ; Jehovah (is his) God; explanation: Joel , the name of twelve Israelites translation:Joel.

Joash = "given by the Lord" 1) son of king Ahaziah and the 8th king of Judah 2) son of king Jehoahaz and the 12th king of the northern kingdom of Israel 3) father of Gideon 4) a son of king Ahab 5) a descendant of Shelah the son of Judah; either the son of Shelah or the son of Jokim 6) son of Shemaah of Gibeah who resorted to David at Ziklag exegesis:or יֹאָשׁ H ; (2 Chronicles 24:1), a form of יְהוֹאָשׁ H3060 ; explanation: Joash , the name of six Israelites translation:Joash.

Job = "persecuted" 1) the 3rd son of Issachar also called 'Jashub' exegesis:perhaps a form of יוֹבָב H3103 , but more probably by erroneous transcription for יָשׁוּב H3437 ; explanation: Job , an Israelite translation:Job.

Jobab = "a desert" 1) the last in order of the sons of Joktan, a descendant of Shem 2) one of the kings of Edom 3) the king of Madon, located in northern Canaan, and one of the kings who opposed Joshua during the conquest and who were routed at Meron 4) head of a Benjamite house 5) another Benjamite exegesis:from יָבַב H2980 ; howler; explanation: Jobab , the name of two Israelites and of three foreigners translation:Jobab.

[G:references: G859,G2413,G4536] 1) ram, ram's horn, trumpet, cornet 1a) ram (only in combination) 1a1) ram's horn, trumpet 1b) jubilee year (marked by the blowing of cornets) (meton) exegesis:or יֹבֵל H ; apparently from יָבַל H2986 ; explanation: the blast of a horn (from its continuous sound); specifically, the signal of the silver trumpets ; hence, the instrument itself and the festival thus introduced translation:jubile, ram's horn, trumpet.
[G:references: G2429] 1) stream exegesis:from יָבַל H2986 ; explanation: a stream translation:river.
Jubal = "stream" 1) the son of Lamech by Adah and the inventor of musical instruments exegesis:from יָבַל H2986 ; stream; explanation: Jubal , an antediluvian translation:Jubal.

Jozabad or Josabad = "Jehovah has endowed" 1) a Korhite Levite, 2nd son of Obededom, and one of the porters of the temple and of the storehouse there in the time of David 2) one of David's mighty warriors 3) another of David's mighty warriors 4) four other priests or Levites exegesis:a form of יְהוֹזָבָד H3075 ; explanation: Jozabad , the name of ten Israelites translation:Josabad, Jozabad.

Jozachar = "Jehovah remembers" 1) a servant of king Joash of Judah and one of his murderers 1a) also called 'Zabad' exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and זָכַר H2142 ; Jehovah-remembered; explanation: Jozacar , an Israelite translation:Jozachar.

Joha = "Jehovah gives life" 1) one of the sons of Beriah the Benjamite 2) the Tizite, one of David's mighty warriors exegesis:probably from יְהֹוָה H3068 and a variation of חָיָה H2421 ; Jehovah-revived; explanation: Jocha , the name of two Israelites translation:Joha.

[G:references: G2491] Johanan = "Jehovah has graced" 1) a priest during the high priesthood of Joiakim who returned with Zerubbabel 2) a Jewish captain after the fall of Jerusalem 3) the eldest son of king Josiah 4) a post-exilic prince of the line of David 5) father of Azariah, priest in Solomon's time 6) a Benjamite, one of David's mighty warriors 7) a Gadite, one of David's mighty warriors 8) a returning exile exegesis:a form of יְהוֹחָנָן H3076 ; explanation: Jochanan , the name of nine Israelites translation:Johanan.

Joiada or Jehoiada = "Jehovah knows" 1) son of Paseah, who assisted to repair the old gate of Jerusalem 2) son of the high priest Eliashib in the time of Nehemiah exegesis:a form of יְהוֹיָדָע H3077 ; explanation: Jojada , the name of two Israelites translation:Jehoiada, Joiada.

Jehoiachin = "Jehovah establishes" 1) king of Judah, son of Jehoiakim, and the next to last king of Judah before the Babylonian captivity; kingship lasted for 3 months and 10 days before he surrendered to Nebuchadnezzar who took him to Babylon and imprisoned him for 36 years when he was finally released exegesis:a form of יְהוֹיָכִין H3078 ; explanation: Jojakin , an Israelite king translation:Jehoiachin.

Joiakim or Jehoiakim = "Jehovah raises up" 1) a priest and son of Jeshua the high priest exegesis:a form of יְהוֹיָקִים H3079 ; explanation: Jojakim , an Israelite translation:Joiakim. Compare יוֹקִים H3137 .

Joiarib = "Jehovah contends" 1) the head of the 1st of the 24 courses of priests in David's time 2) a teacher in the time of Ezra 3) a Judaite in the time of Nehemiah exegesis:a form of יְהוֹיָרִיב H3080 ; explanation: Jojarib , the name of four Israelites translation:Joiarib.

Jochebed = "Jehovah is glory" 1) the daughter of Levi, wife and at the same time aunt of Amram, and the mother of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 contracted and כָּבַד H3513 ; Jehovah-gloried; explanation: Jokebed , the mother of Moses translation:Jochebed.

Jucal = "Jehovah is able" 1) a son of Shelemiah and a servant of king Zedekiah whom he sent to Jeremiah to entreat his prayers and advice exegesis:a form of יְהוּכַל H3081 ; explanation: Jukal , an Israelite translation:Jucal.

[G:references: G746,G979,G1074,G1538,G2094,G2167,G2222,G2250,G2250,G2250,G2250,G2520,G2540,G3568,G3598,G4594,G5550] 1) day, time, year 1a) day (as opposed to night) 1b) day (24 hour period) 1b1) as defined by evening and morning in Genesis 1 1b2) as a division of time 1b2a) a working day, a day's journey 1c) days, lifetime (pl.) 1d) time, period (general) 1e) year 1f) temporal references 1f1) today 1f2) yesterday 1f3) tomorrow exegesis:from an unused root meaning to be hot; explanation: a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an associated term), (often used adverb) translation:age, [phrase] always, [phrase] chronicals, continually(-ance), daily, ((birth-), each, to) day, (now a, two) days (agone), [phrase] elder, [idiom] end, [phrase] evening, [phrase] (for) ever(-lasting, -more), [idiom] full, life, as (so) long as (... live), (even) now, [phrase] old, [phrase] outlived, [phrase] perpetually, presently, [phrase] remaineth, [idiom] required, season, [idiom] since, space, then, (process of) time, [phrase] as at other times, [phrase] in trouble, weather, (as) when, (a, the, within a) while (that), [idiom] whole ([phrase] age), (full) year(-ly), [phrase] younger.

1) day 2) day always refers to a twenty-four hour period when the word is modified by a definite or cardinal number exegesis:(Aramaic) corresponding to יוֹם H3117 ; explanation: a day translation:day (by day), time.
[G:references: G2250] adv 1) by day, in the daytime subst 2) daytime exegesis:from יוֹם H3117 ; explanation: daily translation:daily, (by, in the) day(-time).

Javan = "Ionia" or "Greece" n pr m 1) a son of Japheth and grandson of Noah n pr loc 2) Greece, Ionia, Ionians 2a) location of descendants of Javan exegesis:probably from the same as יַיִן H3196 ; effervescing (i.e. hot and active); explanation: Javan , the name of a son of Joktan, and of the race (Ionians, i.e. Greeks) descended from him, with their territory; also of a place in Arabia translation:Javan.
[G:references: G5208] 1) mire, mirer exegesis:from the same as יַיִן H3196 ; explanation:properly, dregs (as effervescing); hence, mud translation:mire, miry.

Jonadab or Jehonadab = "Jehovah is willing" 1) a son of Rechab, chief of the Rechabites, in the time of Jehu and Ahab 2) a nephew of David exegesis:a form of יְהוֹנָדָב H3082 ; explanation: Jonadab , the name of an Israelite and of a Rechabite translation:Jonadab.
[G:references: G4058] 1) dove, pigeon exegesis:probably from the same as יַיִן H3196 ; explanation: a dove (apparently from the warmth of their mating) translation:dove, pigeon.

x-typo:xlit Yônâh corrected to [G:references: G2495,G920] Jonah = "dove" 1) son of Amittai and a native of Gath-hepher; 5th of the minor prophets who prophesied during the reign of Jeroboam II and whom God sent also to prophecy to Nineveh exegesis:the same as יוֹנָה H3123 ; explanation: Jonah , an Israelite translation:Jonah.
1) Grecian, Ionian exegesis:patronymically from יָוֵן H3121 ; explanation: a Jevanite , or descendant of Javan translation:Grecian.

[G:references: G3813,G3813] 1) sucker, suckling, sapling, young plant exegesis:active participle of יָנַק H3243 ; explanation: a sucker ; hence, a twig (of a tree felled and sprouting) translation:tender plant.
[G:references: G2798] 1) young plant, twig, young shoot exegesis:feminine of יוֹנֵק H3126 ; explanation: a sprout translation:(tender) branch, young twig.

Jonath-elem-recho-kim = "the silent dove of far-off places" 1) meaning uncertain 1a) probably name of melody 1b) only used in title of Ps 56:1 exegesis:from יוֹנָה H3123 and אֵלֶם H482 and the plural of רָחוֹק H7350 ; explanation: dove of (the) silence (i.e. dumb Israel) of (i.e. among) distances (i.e. strangers); the title of a ditty (used for a name of its melody) translation:Jonath-elem-rechokim.

Jonathan or Jehonathan = "Jehovah has given" 1) a son of king Saul and a friend of David 2) a son of the high priest Abiathar and the last descendant of Eli of whom we hear 3) one of David's mighty warriors 4) a Levite and father of Zechariah, a priest who blew the trumpet at the dedication of the wall 5) a son of Kareah and a brother of Johanan; a Judaite captain after the fall of Jerusalem 6) another Judaite 7) father of Ebed in the time of Ezra 8) son of Asahel in the time of Ezra 9) a priest of the family of Melicu in the time of Nehemiah 10) son of Joiada and his successor to the high priesthood in the time of Nehemiah exegesis:a form of יְהוֹנָתָן H3083 ; explanation: Jonathan , the name of ten Israelites translation:Jonathan.

[G:references: G2501] Joseph = "Jehovah has added" 1) the eldest son of Jacob by Rachel 2) father of Igal, who represented the tribe of Issachar among the spies 3) a son of Asaph 4) a man who took a foreign wife in the time of Ezra 5) a priest of the family of Shebaniah in the time of Nehemiah exegesis:future of יָסַף H3254 ; let him add (or perhaps simply active participle adding); explanation: Joseph , the name of seven Israelites translation:Joseph. Josiphiah = "Jehovah adds" 1) father or ancestor of Shelomith who returned with Ezra exegesis:from active participle of יָסַף H3254 and יָהּ H3050 ; Jah (is) adding; explanation: Josiphjah , an Israelite translation:Josiphiah.

Joelah = "may he avail" 1) son of Jeroham of Gedor and one of David's mighty warriors exegesis:perhaps feminine active participle of יַעַל H3276 ; furthermore; explanation: Joelah , an Israelite translation:Joelah.
Joed = "Jehovah is witness" 1) a Benjamite, son of Pedaiah exegesis:apparently the active participle of יָעַד H3259 ; appointer; explanation: Joed , an Israelite translation:Joed.

Joezer = "Jehovah is help" 1) a Korhite, one of David's mighty warriors who joined him at Ziklag exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and עֵזֶר H5828 ; Jehovah (is his) help; explanation: Joezer , an Israelite translation:Joezer.

Joash = "Jehovah hastens" 1) a Benjamite, son of Becher 2) one of the officers in David's court exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and עוּשׁ H5789 ; Jehovahhastened; explanation: Joash , the name of two Israelites translation:Joash.

Jozadak = "Jehovah is righteous" 1) grandson of the high priest Hilkiah; son of the high priest Seraiah; and father of the high priest Joshua; he never attained the office of high priest himself because he was carried captive to Babylon by Nebuchadnezzar exegesis:a form of יְהוֹצָדָק H3087 ; explanation: Jotsadak , an Israelite translation:Jozadak.
[G:references: G4406,G885] Jokim = "Jehovah raises up" 1) a descendant of Judah exegesis:a form of יוֹיָקִים H3113 ; explanation: Jokim , an Israelite translation:Jokim.

1) early rain, autumn shower 1a) rain which falls in Palestine from late October until early December exegesis:active participle of יָרָה H3384 ; explanation: sprinkling ; hence, a sprinkling (or autumnal showers) translation:first rain, former (rain).
Jorah = "he instructs" 1) the head or ancestor of a family who returned from Babylon with Ezra exegesis:from יָרָה H3384 ; rainy; explanation: Jorah , an Israelite translation:Jorah.

Jorai = "Jehovah has taught me" 1) one of the Gadites dwelling at Gilead in Bashan in the reign of king Jotham of Judah exegesis:from יָרָה H3384 ; rainy; explanation: Jorai , an Israelite translation:Jorai.

[G:references: G2496] Joram = "Jehovah is exalted" 1) son of king Jehoshaphat of Judah and himself king of Judah for 8 years; his wife was the wicked Athaliah who was probably the instigator for his returning the nation of Judah to the worship of Baal 2) son of king Ahab of the northern kingdom of Israel and king of Israel himself for 12 years; he was murdered by Jehu on the plot of land for which his father had murdered Naboth thus fulfilling the prophecy of Elijah to the very letter 3) a Levite in the time of David and an ancestor of Shelomith 4) son of Toi, king of Hamath exegesis:a form of יְהוֹרָם H3088 ; explanation: Joram , the name of three Israelites and one Syrian translation:Joram.
Jushab-hesed = "whose love is returned" 1) son of Zerubbabel exegesis:from שׁוּב H7725 and חֵסֵד H2617 ; kindness will be returned; explanation: Jushab-Chesed , an Israelite translation:Jushab-hesed.
Josibiah = "Jehovah causes to dwell" 1) a Simeonite, father of Jehu exegesis:from יָשַׁב H3427 and יָהּ H3050 ; Jehovah will cause to dwell; explanation: Josibjah , an Israelite translation:Josibiah.
Joshah = "Jehovah makes equal" 1) a prince of the house of Simeon exegesis:probably a form of יוֹשַׁוְיָה H3145 ; explanation: Joshah , an Israelite translation:Joshah.

Joshaviah = "Jehovah makes equal" 1) son of Elnaam and one of David's mighty warriors exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and שָׁוָה H7737 ; Jehovah-set; explanation: Joshavjah , an Israelite translation:Joshaviah. Compare יוֹשָׁה H3144 .

Joshaphat or Jehoshaphat = "Jehovah judges" n pr m 1) one of David's mighty warriors 2) a priest and trumpeter in the time of David exegesis:a form of יְהוֹשָׁפָט H3092 ; explanation: Joshaphat , an Israelite translation:Joshaphat.

[G:references: G2488] Jotham = "Jehovah is perfect" 1) son of king Uzziah of Judah by Jerushah; king of Judah for 16 years and contemporary with Isaiah and king Pekah of Israel 2) son of Jahdai and a descendant of Caleb and Judah 3) youngest son of Gideon who escaped from the massacre of his brothers exegesis:from יְהֹוָה H3068 and תָּם H8535 ; Jehovah (is) perfect; explanation: Jotham , the name of three Israelites translation:Jotham.

n m 1) superiority, advantage, excess adv 2) excess, better conj 3) besides, moreover, more exegesis:active participle of יָתַר H3498 ; explanation:properly, redundant ; hence, over and above , as adjective, noun, adverb or conjunction translation:better, more(-over), over, profit.

Jeziel = "the assembly of God" 1) a Benjamite and one of David's mighty warriors who joined him at Ziklag exegesis:from an unused root (meaning to sprinkle) and אֵל H410 ; sprinkled of God; explanation: Jezavel , an Israelite translation:Jeziel (from the margin).

Jeziah = "Jehovah sprinkles" 1) a descendant of Parosh who took a foreign wife in the time of Ezra exegesis:from the same as the first part of יְזַוְאֵל H3149 and יָהּ H3050 ; sprinkled of Jah; explanation: Jizzijah , an Israelite translation:Jeziah.

Jaziz = "He makes prominent" 1) a Hagarite, officer of David in charge of the flocks exegesis:from the same as זִיז H2123 ; he will make prominent; explanation: Jaziz , an Israelite translation:Jaziz.

Jezliah = "whom God will preserve" 1) a Benjamite of the sons of Elpaal exegesis:perhaps from an unused root (meaning to draw up); he will draw out; explanation: Jizliah , an Israelite translation:Jezliah.

Jezaniah = "Jehovah has listened" 1) son of Hoshaiah the Maachathite and one of the captains who escaped from Jerusalem during the last siege of the Babylonians; one of the first to return to Gedaliah at Mizpah where he took a prominent part after the assassination of Gedaliah exegesis:or יְזַנְיָהוּ H ; probably for יַאֲזַנְיָה H2970 ; explanation: Jezanjah , an Israelite translation:Jezaniah.
[G:references: G970] 1) sweat, perspiration exegesis:from an unused root mean to ooze; explanation: sweat , i.e. (by implication) a sweating dress translation:any thing that causeth sweat.

Izrahite = "descendant of Zerah" 1) a descendant of Zerah of Judah exegesis:a variation for אֶזְרָחִי H250 ; explanation: a Jizrach (i.e. Ezrachite or Zarchite) or descendant of Zerach translation:Izrahite.

Izrahiah or Jezrahiah = "Jehovah will shine" 1) a chieftain of Issachar 2) a Levite and leader of the chorus at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem under Nehemiah exegesis:from זָרַח H2224 and יָהּ H3050 ; Jah will shine; explanation: Jizrachjah , the name of two Israelites translation:Izrahiah, Jezrahiah.

Jezreel = "God sows" n pr m 1) a descendant of the father or founder of Etam of Judah 2) first son of Hosea the prophet n pr loc 3) a city in the Nekeb of Judah 4) a city in Issachar on the northwest spur of Mount Gilboa exegesis:from זָרַע H2232 and אֵל H410 ; God will sow; explanation: Jizreel , the name of two places in Palestine and of two Israelites translation:Jezreel.
Jezreelite = see Jezreel "sown of God" 1) an inhabitant of Jezreel exegesis:patronymically from יִזְרְעֵאל H3157 ; explanation: a Jizreelite or native of Jizreel translation:Jezreelite.

Jezreelitess = see Jezreel "sown of God" 1) a female inhabitant of Jezreel 1a) used only of Ahinoam, a wife of David exegesis:feminine of יִזְרְעֵאלִי H3158 ; explanation: a Jezreelitess translation:Jezreelitess.
Jehubbah = "hidden" 1) a man of Asher, son of Shomer or Shamer of the house of Beriah exegesis:from חָבָה H2247 ; hidden; explanation: Jechubbah , an Israelite translation:Jehubbah.
1) to join, unite, be joined, be united 1a) (Qal) to be united 1b) (Piel) to unite exegesis:a primitive root; explanation: to be (or become) one translation:join, unite.

[G:references: G3661,G3661] n m 1) union, unitedness adv 2) together, altogether, all together, alike exegesis:from יָחַד H3161 ; explanation:properly, a unit , i.e. (adverb) unitedly translation:alike, at all (once), both, likewise, only, (al-) together, withal.

Jahdo = "His unity" 1) a Gileadite, son of Buz and father of Jeshishai exegesis:from יַחַד H3162 with pronominal suffix; his unity, i.e. (adverb) together; explanation: Jachdo , an Israelite translation:Jahdo.
Jahdiel = "my unity is God" 1) a chief of Manasseh on the east of the Jordan exegesis:from יַחַד H3162 and אֵל H410 ; unity of God; explanation: Jachdiel , an Israelite translation:Jahdiel.

Jehdeiah = "Jehovah is unity" 1) a Levite in the time of David 2) a Meronothite, officer of David in charge of the she-asses exegesis:from יַחַד H3162 and יָהּ H3050 ; unity of Jah; explanation: Jechdijah , the name of two Israelites translation:Jehdeiah.

Jahaziel = "beheld of God" 1) a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag 2) a priest in the reign of David who helped move the ark 3) a Kohathite Levite, 3rd son of Hebron 4) a Levite, son of Zechariah, of the sons of Asaph, in the reign of Jehoshaphat 5) an ancestor of one of the families who returned from exile with Ezra exegesis:from חָזָה H2372 and אֵל H410 ; beheld of God; explanation: Jachaziel , the name of five Israelites translation:Jahaziel, Jahziel.

Jahaziah = "Jehovah views" 1) son of Tikvah, apparently a priest and leader during the time of Ezra exegesis:from חָזָה H2372 and יָהּ H3050 ; Jah will behold; explanation: Jachzejah , an Israelite translation:Jahaziah.

Ezekiel or Jehezekel = "God strengthens" 1) son of Buzi and a priest and prophet; author of the book by his name; taken captive with Jehoiachin and exiled in Babylon where he prophesied for the next 22 years 2) a priest in charge of the 20th course in the time of David exegesis:from חָזַק H2388 and אֵל H410 ; God will strengthen; explanation: Jechezkel , the name of two Israelites translation:Ezekiel, Jehezekel.

Hezekiah or Jehizkiah = "Jehovah has made strong" 1) son of king Ahazby Abi or Abijah and the 12th king of Judah; his reign was characterised by his godly conduct; reigned for 29 years 2) head of a family of returning exiles in the time of Nehemiah 3) an Ephraimite in the time of Ahaz exegesis:or יְחִזְקִיָּהוּ H ; from יְרוּשָׁא H3388 and יָהּ H3050 ; strengthened of Jah; explanation: Jechizkijah , the name of five Israelites translation:Hezekiah, Jehizkiah. Compare חִזְקִיָּה H2396 .
Jahzerah = "whom God leads back" 1) a priest of the house of Immer exegesis:from the same as חֲזִיר H2386 ; perhaps protection; explanation: Jachzerah , an Israelite translation:Jahzerah.

Jehiel = "God lives" 1) a Levite and one of the chief musicians in the time of David 2) a Gershonite Levite who had charge of the treasures in the time of David 3) son of Hachmoni and an officer of David and tutor of David's sons 4) a son of king Jehoshaphat of Judah put to death by his brother Jehoram 5) Hemanite who took part in the restoration of king Hezekiah 6) a Levite and overseer in the time of Hezekiah 7) a ruler of the temple during the reforms of Josiah 8) father of Obadiah of the sons of Joab in the time of Ezra 9) father of Shechaniah of the sons of Elam in the time of Ezra 10) a son of Elam who put away his foreign wife in the time of Ezra 11) a priest of the sons of Harim who also had to put away his foreign wife in the time of Ezra exegesis:or (2 Chronicles 29:14) יְחַוְאֵל H ; from חָיָה H2421 and אֵל H410 ; God will live; explanation: Jechiel (or Jechavel), the name of eight Israelites translation:Jehiel.

Jehieli = "God shall save alive" or "swept away of God" 1) a Gershonite Levite of the family of Laadan exegesis:patronymically from יְחִיאֵל H3171 ; explanation: a Jechielite or descendant of Jechiel translation:Jehieli.

Jehiah = "Jehovah lives" 1) doorkeeper of the ark at the time of its establishment in Jerusalem exegesis:from חָיָה H2421 and יָהּ H3050 ; Jah will live; explanation: Jechijah , an Israelite translation:Jehiah.

Jahleel = "God waits" 1) the 3rd of the 3 sons of Zebulun; founder of the family of the Jahleelites exegesis:from יָחַל H3176 and אֵל H410 ; expectant of God; explanation: Jachleel , an Israelite translation:Jahleel.

Jahleelites = "the hope of God" 1) descendants of Jahleel of the tribe of Zebulun exegesis:patronymically from יַחְלְאֵל H3177 ; explanation: a Jachleelite or descendant of Jachleel translation:Jahleelites.

[G:references: G2627] 1) flood, deluge. 1a) Noah's flood that submerged the entire planet earth under water for about a year ++++ Some think Noah's flood was only local. However the description of it found in Gen. 6 through 8 makes this patently absurd. If it was local, Noah had 120 years to migrate out of the area to safe ground! Why waste all that effort building a ship? He only had to move less than 1500 feet a day to reach the farthest point on the globe! With the possible exception of Ps 29:10, this word always refers to Noah's flood. The real reason for insisting on a local flood is the acceptance of evolution with its long geological ages. Most holding that view are not willing to allow a global world wide flood to have happened less than 5000 years ago. To admit such eliminates the need for the geological ages for most of the geological column would have been rapidly laid down by Noah's flood. exegesis:from יָבַל H2986 in the sense of flowing; explanation: a deluge translation:flood.

[G:references: G1085,G3313,G3665,G4133,G4386] 1) kind, sometimes a species (usually of animals) ++++ Groups of living organisms belong in the same created "kind" if they have descended from the same ancestral gene pool. This does not preclude new species because this represents a partitioning of the original gene pool. Information is lost or conserved not gained. A new species could arise when a population is isolated and inbreeding occurs. By this definition a new species is not a new "kind" but a further partitioning of an existing "kind". exegesis:from an unused root meaning to portion out; explanation: a sort , i.e. species translation:kind. Compare מִן H4480 .

[G:references: G2847,G2848,G4663] 1) worm, scarlet stuff, crimson 1a) worm-the female 'coccus ilicis' 1b) scarlet stuff, crimson, scarlet 1b1) the dye made from the dried body of the female of the worm "coccus ilicis" 2) worm, maggot 2a) worm, grub 2b) the worm "coccus ilicis" ++++ When the female of the scarlet worm species was ready to give birth to her young, she would attach her body to the trunk of a tree, fixing herself so firmly and permanently that she would never leave again. The eggs deposited beneath her body were thus protected until the larvae were hatched and able to enter their own life cycle. As the mother died, the crimson fluid stained her body and the surrounding wood. From the dead bodies of such female scarlet worms, the commercial scarlet dyes of antiquity were extracted. What a picture this gives of Christ, dying on the tree, shedding His precious blood that He might "bring many sons unto glory" (Heb 2:10)! He died for us, that we might live through him! Ps 22:6 describes such a worm and gives us this picture of Christ. (cf.
